content:
💡 video-use | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: Automating video editing through natural language commands processed by AI coding agents.
✨ Key Features: • Cuts filler words and dead space • Auto color grades segments • Adds 30ms audio fades at cuts • Burns in customizable subtitles • Generates animation overlays
📖 Summary: video-use is an open-source Python tool that leverages AI coding agents to automate video editing tasks. It processes raw footage by cutting filler words, applying color grading, adding audio fades, burning in customizable subtitles, and generating animation overlays, all while self-evaluating the output.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🚀 ratty | Rust
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide an enhanced and visually rich terminal experience by enabling 3D graphics and GPU-accelerated rendering within the terminal interface.
✨ Key Features: • GPU-backed text rendering • Traditional 2D and new 3D mode • Inline 3D objects • Image support (Kitty Graphics Protocol) • Customizable spinning rat cursor
📖 Summary: Ratty is a GPU-accelerated terminal emulator built with Rust and Ratatui, offering traditional 2D text rendering alongside a novel 3D mode. It supports inline 3D objects and image rendering via the Kitty Graphics Protocol, providing a unique visual experience for terminal applications.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🌟 lay-public | Rust
🎯 Primary Use Case: Correcting the last typed word to the correct keyboard layout (e.g., Russian/English) with a double Shift press on Linux desktops, especially GNOME Wayland.
✨ Key Features: • Double Shift to fix the last word typed in the wrong layout • Works directly in applications without clipboard use • Supports GNOME Wayland via a Shell extension • Experimental support for KDE/Plasma and X11 backends • CLI for terminal text conversion
📖 Summary: Lay is a Rust-based utility for Linux desktops, primarily GNOME Wayland, that corrects typing errors caused by incorrect keyboard layouts. It allows users to fix the last typed word by double-pressing the Shift key, seamlessly switching it to the intended layout without manual copy-pasting.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🌟 hackingtool | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide a unified and easy-to-use interface for accessing and managing a wide array of hacking and penetration testing tools for security professionals.
✨ Key Features: • All-in-one hacking tool with 185+ tools • Organized into 20 categories • Supports web attacks, password attacks, wireless attacks, etc. • Python 3.10+ based • OS-aware menus (Linux/macOS)
📖 Summary: HackingTool is a comprehensive, all-in-one Python-based toolkit designed for security researchers and penetration testers. It aggregates over 185 tools across 20 categories, offering functionalities for web attacks, password attacks, wireless attacks, and more, with features like smart search, tag filtering, and automated installation.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🔥 davinci-resolve-mcp | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: To enable AI assistants to control and automate post-production workflows within DaVinci Resolve through natural language commands.
✨ Key Features: • Complete coverage of DaVinci Resolve Scripting API • Integration with AI assistants (Claude, Cursor, Windsurf) • 30 context-efficient tools for post-production control • Guarded kernel workflows for safe operations (probing, reporting, dry-runs)
📖 Summary: This Python-based MCP server provides comprehensive integration with the DaVinci Resolve Scripting API, enabling AI assistants to control post-production workflows. It offers 30 context-efficient tools for tasks like timeline editing, media management, rendering, and color grading.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source
